content.title: Your Dentist Knows You Got Filler
content.preview: A new oral radiology study shows HA filler on dental X-rays. Plus: the zinc-carnosine trial gastroenterologists are quietly obsessed with, and Chanel reformulated a lip product nobody asked them to touch.
content.but_first: Dental X-rays are now accidentally mapping your filler history, and radiologists just published the receipts.

quote_of_day.text: Beauty is the promise of happiness.
quote_of_day.attribution: — Stendhal, stolen by every brand deck since 1822

injection_report.headline: Your Panoramic X-Ray Is Snitching on Your Filler
injection_report.body: **What's going on:** A study published March 31 in *Dentomaxillofacial Radiology* analyzed 420 panoramic radiographs and found that hyaluronic acid filler in the nasolabial folds and lips showed up as distinct radiopaque masses in 23% of patients who'd had injections within the past 18 months. Radiologists flagged several as "pathology" before clinical history clarified. **What it means:** HA fillers aren't supposed to be radiopaque, but cross-linked gels at higher concentrations scatter X-ray beams just enough to mimic soft-tissue lesions. If your dentist orders a CBCT scan, the detail gets even more obvious. This isn't dangerous, but it creates diagnostic confusion and unnecessary biopsies. **What's next:** Tell your dentist you have filler. Seriously. One sentence saves everyone a referral.
injection_report.highlight: Before your next dental imaging appointment, disclose every injectable you've had in the past two years. It belongs on your intake form now.
injection_report.related: The full Dentomaxillofacial Radiology study on HA filler visibility in panoramic and CBCT imaging

put_it_in_your_mouth.headline: Zinc-Carnosine Just Got Its Biggest Trial Yet
put_it_in_your_mouth.body: **What's going on:** A randomized, placebo-controlled trial out of Kyoto University, published online March 30 in *Gut*, gave 75 mg of zinc-carnosine twice daily to 196 adults with functional dyspepsia for 12 weeks. The treatment group saw a 41% reduction in symptom severity scores versus 18% for placebo. Mucosal integrity markers improved significantly by week 8. **What that means:** Zinc-carnosine (sold as Pepzin GI in the U.S.) stabilizes the gastric mucosal lining without suppressing acid. This is the first large RCT to confirm what Japanese GI docs have prescribed for decades. If you're on a PPI you'd rather quit, this is the conversation to bring to your gastroenterologist.
put_it_in_your_mouth.highlight: Zinc-carnosine is not the same as zinc gluconate or zinc picolinate. The carnosine chelate is the whole point. Look for Pepzin GI specifically.
put_it_in_your_mouth.cheat_meal: A proper Japanese egg sandwich (tamago sando) from Konbi or your city's closest equivalent. Kewpie mayo, white milk bread, soft-scrambled eggs. The gut lining deserves softness this week.
put_it_in_your_mouth.related: The full Kyoto University RCT on zinc-carnosine and functional dyspepsia in Gut

lip_lab.headline: Chanel Rouge Allure Velvet Got a Quiet Reformulation and the Internet Is Split
lip_lab.body: **What's going on:** Chanel updated the Rouge Allure Velvet formula as of its Spring 2026 batch (confirmed by component codes surfacing on Xiaohongshu and verified by Beauty Independent on April 1). The new version swaps synthetic beeswax for a plant-derived alternative and adds tocopheryl acetate. **Why it matters:** The original's matte grip came partly from that beeswax-silicone matrix. Early testers report a slightly dewier settle and less feathering resistance at the lip line. Chanel hasn't acknowledged the change publicly. **The verdict:** If you loved the old version's dry-down, buy your shade on markdown while batch codes still match. The new formula is fine, just different. It's a soft matte now, not a true one.
lip_lab.highlight: Check the batch code on the bottom of your tube at checkcosmetic.net. Codes starting with 6A are the new formula.
lip_lab.related: Beauty Independent's ingredient comparison of the old and new Chanel Rouge Allure Velvet formulas

lips_in_6.text: Galderma filed a supplemental FDA application on April 1 to expand Restylane Kysse's indication to include vertical lip lines, not just lip volume. Approval could land by Q3.
A March 31 meta-analysis in *Nutrients* found that daily pomegranate extract (500 mg punicalagins) reduced gingival inflammation markers by 29% across four RCTs. Your gums want you to eat the seeds.
Tower 28 just dropped a limited ShineOn Lip Jelly shade, Cashew, a 90s-nude with actual staying power, exclusive to their DTC site through April 10.
Researchers at MIT published a preprint on April 2 describing a microneedle patch that delivers botulinum toxin transdermally. Animal data only, but the mechanism is real.
The EU's SCCS flagged butylphenyl methylpropional (Lilial) in three lip balms still sold on Amazon UK as of this week. Check your imported products.

Sabrina Carpenter's Espresso Tour opening night (March 30, Las Vegas) featured a custom lip look by makeup artist Carolina Gonzalez using a lip stain layered under gloss. TikTok already has 8M views on dupes.

rotation_1.text: Dr. Dennis Gross DRx SpectraLite LipWare Pro. Yes, the name is absurd. But 4 minutes of red and infrared LED on your lips three times a week genuinely boosts collagen density over time. We've been using it since January and our vertical lip lines are quieter. Not gone, quieter. That's honest.

Sakara Life Metabolism Super Powder. We put it in our morning water. It tastes like slightly fancy dirt. But the sulforaphane and moringa blend keeps energy steady through noon without the caffeine jitter, and the gut support tracks with what we've been reading in the zinc-carnosine literature. *Keep scrolling…more recs below*

quick_hits.label: Best Kiss
quick_hits.text: Glossier quietly donating 10,000 units of Balm Dotcom to NYC public school nurses this week, no press release, just a restock confirmation email someone leaked. That's how you do it.

quick_hits.label: Keep It Out
quick_hits.text: A Brazilian Blowout dupe sold on TikTok Shop was flagged by the EWG on April 1 for containing 0.08% formaldehyde, well above the EU limit and enough to irritate mucous membranes. If you bought "Silk Keratin Pro Treatment" in the last 30 days, stop using it.

A new *JAMA Network Open* letter published April 2 reported that patients who received neuromodulators within 48 hours of a COVID booster had a 6x higher rate of temporary eyelid ptosis. Small sample (n=34), but the timing signal is worth noting for your next appointment.
Ritual launched a reformulated Essential for Women 18+ multivitamin on April 1 that finally includes 15 mg of zinc bisglycinate, up from zero. The old formula's biggest gap, filled without fanfare.

on_our_calendar.text: April 4: Sephora's Spring Savings Event opens for Rouge members. 20% off everything, including the Chanel lip counter. Old batch codes still in stock at most locations.
April 5: The American Academy of Dermatology's Innovation Academy virtual session on next-gen neuromodulators streams free to non-members for the first time.
April 7: World Health Day. This year's WHO theme is "My Health, My Right," with a specific sub-campaign on oral health access in low-income countries.

rotation_2.text: Vanicream Lip Protectant SPF 30. Boring packaging. Zero fragrance. The only lip SPF that doesn't migrate, taste like sunscreen, or pill under lipstick. We buy four at a time and stash them like emergency rations.

Cozy Earth Silk Pillowcase. Your lip filler, your retinol, your night lip mask: all of them work better when they're not being dragged off your face by cotton. Silk reduces friction by roughly 43% versus sateen. We tested. We measured. We sound unhinged. We don't care. *We only feature things we'd actually use on our own face.*

last_word.quote: The mouth is the gatekeeper of the body and the traitor of the soul.
last_word.attribution: — Hippocrates, approximately, via a very loose translation that still holds up
